pullRequest.title: Add SANDBOX_SMOKE_TEST.md for isolated Cloudflare Sandbox workflow validation
pullRequest.author: fashn-internal-agent-dev[bot]

overview.headline: Adds a smoke-test document to validate the Cloudflare Sandbox workflow produces clean, reviewable changesets.
overview.body:
## Purpose

This PR introduces a single new file, `SANDBOX_SMOKE_TEST.md`, whose sole purpose is to confirm that the isolated Cloudflare Sandbox environment can boot, accept edits, and produce a clean, reviewable changeset with no unintended side effects.  The file itself is a brief marker — it does not affect any runtime behavior, configuration, or tests.

## Scope

**In scope:**
- Creation of `SANDBOX_SMOKE_TEST.md` (3 lines of markdown).

**Out of scope:**
- Any changes to existing source code, configuration, tests, or documentation.
- Any functional or behavioral changes to the application.

## Major Decisions

- **Minimal footprint:** The document is kept to a single heading and one sentence to avoid any risk of accidental side effects during the validation run.
- **Isolated validation:** No other files are touched, ensuring the smoke test result is unambiguous — if the sandbox workflow is broken, only this file will be missing or malformed.

## Areas

- Documentation
- CI / Sandbox workflow
majorDecisions: Minimal document (3 lines) to reduce risk of unintended side effects during validation.
No other files modified, making the smoke test result unambiguous.

review.summary: Code Beat did not find clear actionable issues in this pull request.
